title: Large values are rounded when exporting to CSV
description:
I noticed an important regression introduced in Virtuoso v7.2.1 (Jun 24th 2015): large numeric values are rounded when exporting to CSV format from Virtuoso SPARQL Query Editor (and some other formats too, but CSV is the one I use).
For example, since version 7.2.1, a value of "6341995000" stored in Virtuoso is displayed "6.342e+09" in CSV output. So "6341995000" is rounded to "6342000000".
In the previous tagged version (v7.2.0.1), the actual value (not rounded) was displayed when exporting to CSV.
In TURTLE export format, this is the opposite!
Value "6341995000" was rounded to "6.342e+09" in version 7.2.0.1 and is not rounded anymore since version 7.2.1.
I think that, first of all, numeric values should NOT be rounded at export time. And besides, rounded or unrounded should not depend on the choosen output format...
You can reproduce similar problem from DBPedia (Virtuoso version 07.20.3217 which I assume is v7.2.4.2) with float numbers that are rounded in HTML and CSV format, while you get the right, not rounded value in TURTLE format:
http://dbpedia.org/sparql
Try this query and select CSV or TURTLE export format to see the difference:
SELECT ?s (str(?o) AS ?str_o) ?o (xsd:double(?o) AS ?double_o) (xsd:decimal(?o) AS ?decimal_o) (DATATYPE(?o) AS ?type_o)
WHERE {
 ?s dbp:latestReleaseVersion ?o .
 FILTER (?s IN (<http://dbpedia.org/resource/IBM_Lotus_Freelance_Graphics>, <http://dbpedia.org/resource/IBM_Airline_Control_Program>, <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Corona_(software)>))
}
key_bug_features: Large numeric values are rounded when exporting to CSV
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_large_numbers (id INT PRIMARY KEY, large_value DECIMAL(20, 10));
SELECT id, large_value, (xsd:double(large_value) AS double_value), (xsd:decimal(large_value) AS decimal_value) FROM test_large_numbers;
CREATE TABLE test_null_handling (id INT PRIMARY KEY, nullable_value DECIMAL(20, 10) NULL);
INSERT INTO test_null_handling (id, nullable_value) VALUES (1, NULL);
